items.title: CMS issues guidance on new limits to federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens
items.summary: CMS released a State Health Official letter to help states implement limits on claiming federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP for certain noncitizens starting October 1, 2026. The guidance notes exceptions, including federal matching funds for treatment of emergency medical conditions and continued options for covering lawfully residing children and pregnant people in Medicaid/CHIP.

items.title: Texas SNAP waiver starts restricting sweetened drinks and candy purchases
items.summary: A USDA retailer notice says Texas’ SNAP food restriction waiver took effect April 1, 2026, limiting purchases of sweetened drinks and candy with SNAP EBT. The notice defines sweetened drinks as non-alcoholic beverages made with water that contain five grams or more of added sugar or any amount of artificial sweetener.

items.title: IRS reminds taxpayers: April 15 filing deadline; extensions give until Oct. 15 but taxes still due April 15
items.summary: The IRS says the deadline to file 2025 federal income tax returns is April 15, 2026, and taxpayers who need more time should request an extension by that date. The IRS notes an extension moves the filing deadline to October 15, but it does not extend the time to pay—tax due is still owed by April 15 to avoid penalties and interest.

items.title: IRS: most EITC/ACTC refunds expected by March 2; refund tracker updates for early EITC/ACTC filers by Feb. 21
items.summary: In its announcement opening the 2026 filing season, the IRS said it expects most refunds for filers claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) or the Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) to be available by March 2, 2026 (for direct deposit filers with no other issues). The IRS also said Where’s My Refund? will provide projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC refund filers by February 21, 2026.

items.title: Federal Register: HHS seeks comments on reinstating LIHEAP Household Report information collection
items.summary: The Federal Register published a notice that HHS (including the Administration for Children and Families) is seeking to reinstate the LIHEAP Household Report (OMB No. 0970-0060). Comments are due June 8, 2026.

items.title: USDA: WIC state agencies must allow at least one additional form of fruits/vegetables (frozen/canned/dried) by April 2026
items.summary: USDA FNS says recent WIC food package changes require state agencies to authorize at least one form of fruits and vegetables beyond fresh (frozen, canned, and/or dried) by April 2026. FNS also notes that as of November 2025, 95% of state agencies were already in compliance with this requirement.

items.title: 2026–27 FAFSA is available; online submissions typically processed in 1–3 days
items.summary: Federal Student Aid announced the 2026–27 FAFSA is available for students seeking aid for attendance between July 1, 2026 and June 30, 2027. The announcement says online FAFSA submissions are typically processed in one to three days.

items.title: KFF update: 41 states (including DC) have adopted ACA Medicaid expansion; eligibility up to 138% FPL
items.summary: KFF reports that 41 states (including DC) have adopted the ACA Medicaid expansion, while 10 states have not. KFF notes expansion eligibility is generally up to 138% of the federal poverty level, which it lists as $21,597 for an individual in 2025.

items.title: SSA: Social Security and SSI benefits increase 2.8% in 2026
items.summary: SSA announced a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) for 2026, with Social Security benefits increasing in January 2026 and SSI payments beginning December 31, 2025. SSA also said the taxable maximum will rise to $184,500 (from $176,100).

items.title: USDA posts state list of SNAP food restriction waivers with target implementation dates
items.summary: USDA’s SNAP Food Restriction Waivers page lists states with approved waivers and their target implementation dates. The list includes examples of restrictions such as soft drinks, sweetened carbonated beverages, and candy, depending on the state waiver.

items.title: Federal Student Aid releases 2026–27 Student Aid Handbook Application and Verification Guide
items.summary: Federal Student Aid posted an electronic announcement that the 2026–27 Federal Student Aid Handbook’s Application and Verification Guide is now available. Schools use the guide for FAFSA application processing and verification rules for the 2026–27 cycle.

items.title: Taxpayer Advocate warns new USPS rules could affect whether mailed tax filings count as on time
items.summary: The Taxpayer Advocate Service cautioned that new USPS rules could result in postmarks that are later than the day a return or payment was actually mailed, which matters because the IRS generally treats documents as timely if postmarked on or before the due date. TAS notes the due date for most 2025 individual returns is April 15, 2026.
